sentence: go to wikipedia, search for bezier curves, and scroll all the way down to the end of the article

workflow.name: Start Wikipedia
workflow.description: Navigate to Wikipedia's main page from a fresh tab.
triggers: If the current URL is chrome://new-tab-page/

workflow.name: Search for Bezier Curves
workflow.description: On the Wikipedia homepage, search for 'Bezier curves'.
triggers: If the current URL contains en.wikipedia.org
If a search input with placeholder 'Search Wikipedia' is visible
actions: Type 'Bezier curves' into the search input with placeholder 'Search Wikipedia'

workflow.name: Scroll Bezier Curve Article
workflow.description: Once on the 'Bezier curve' article page, scroll to the very bottom.
triggers: If the current URL contains en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bezier_curve
If a heading with text 'Contents' is visible
actions: Scroll to the bottom of the page

workflow.name: Article Scrolled to End
workflow.description: Confirm the 'Bezier curve' article has been scrolled to its end.
triggers: If the current URL contains en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bezier_curve
If the scroll position is at the bottom of the page
workflow.end_state: Bezier curve article scrolled to end

lint_issues.message: Empty actions with a wait/verification-sounding name -- looks like an intentional 'sit here until something external changes' state. Confirm that's what you meant.
Last state's actions don't mention 'Terminate'. NetGent's web agent needs an explicit terminate instruction to stop cleanly.

sentence: go to en.wikipedia.org, search for a topic, and if a disambiguation page appears pick the first listed meaning, otherwise just read the article

workflow.name: On Browser Home Page
workflow.description: Start the process from a fresh browser tab.
triggers: If the current URL is chrome://new-tab-page/

workflow.name: On Wikipedia Homepage
workflow.description: Search for a generic topic on Wikipedia.
triggers: If the current URL contains en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page
If a search box with placeholder 'Search Wikipedia' is visible

workflow.name: On Disambiguation Page
workflow.description: If the search led to a disambiguation page, click the first listed meaning.
triggers: If the current URL contains en.wikipedia.org/wiki/
If the page title contains '(disambiguation)'
If a div with class 'mw-disambig' is visible
actions: Click the first link that is a direct child of a list item (<li>) on the page

workflow.name: On Article Page
workflow.description: The article page is loaded, either directly after search or after disambiguation.
triggers: If the current URL contains en.wikipedia.org/wiki/
If a main heading (<h1>) is visible
If the page title does NOT contain '(disambiguation)'
workflow.end_state: Article loaded, disambiguation handled if present.

lint_issues.message: Empty actions list, but the name/description doesn't read like a wait state. If this wasn't intentional, NetGent will do nothing here and just re-poll the same triggers.
Last state's actions don't mention 'Terminate'. NetGent's web agent needs an explicit terminate instruction to stop cleanly.
